research_summary: Nurix AI builds enterprise voice + chat AI agents (NuPlay platform). Founded 2024, $42.2M raised (Accel, General Catalyst, Prosus). 84+ person team in Bengaluru. JD explicitly asks for Agentic RAG pipelines, LLM orchestration, low-latency production deployment â€” Amit's exact stack. Team is hands-on with multi-agent orchestration, voice AI evaluation, and production inference engineering. Email pattern: firstname.lastname@nurix.ai (59.3%).
jd_full_text:
Machine Learning Engineer (MLE) â€” Nurix

Key Responsibilities:

Speech and Voice Systems:
- Design, train, and optimize custom ASR/TTS models to overcome limitations of Sarvam ASR
- Develop agents handling interruptions, natural pauses, and real-world voice variability

Core ML/AI Development:
- Contribute to Voice-to-Voice (V2V) systems using state space models (e.g., Mamba, Hamba)
- Develop Agentic RAG pipelines and LLM orchestration for intelligent, context-aware voice agents
- Build self-learning agents for continuous improvement

Engineering and Deployment:
- Collaborate on bringing models into real-time, low-latency production environments
- Focus on scalability, security, and reliability

Collaboration and Research:
- Partner with product/engineering teams
- Stay current with ASR, TTS, NLP, and inference framework advances

Required Qualifications:
- Bachelor's degree in Computer Science, AI/ML, or related field
- 5-10 years of hands-on ML engineering experience, with a focus on speech/NLP systems
- Expertise in ASR, TTS, NLP, LLM fine-tuning, or dialogue systems
- Proficiency in Python and ML frameworks (PyTorch, TensorFlow, JAX)
- Low-latency, real-time ML application design experience
- ML lifecycle, evaluation, and deployment knowledge
- Open-source contributions or research publications
- Prior multilingual or Indian-context ASR/TTS systems work preferred

Note: AI Engineer role also posted separately for 2-4 yrs experience, Bengaluru onsite. Other open roles (May 2026): Prompt Engineer (0-2 yrs), QA Engineer Conversational AI (3-5 yrs), Product Lead (8-10 yrs), Sr. DevOps (8 yrs), Enterprise Architect (10-15 yrs), Product Manager (4-6 yrs).
tailoring_notes: Summary rewritten to mirror JD exact asks: agentic RAG pipelines (LangChain + LangGraph), multi-model LLM orchestration, low-latency inference (16s to 8s), Python/PyTorch/TF -- JD explicitly calls these out. Technical Skills reordered: LLMs and Techniques moved to first position since JD leads with LLM orchestration and Agentic RAG pipelines; LangChain/LangGraph promoted into that section; Languages now second with PyTorch and TensorFlow visible early (JD requires these explicitly). Hardware/IoT row dropped entirely -- JD is pure ML/voice AI, no embedded context. Voicer kept -- Samsung Solve for Tomorrow Top 10 among 70,000+ participants is a strong signal, and on-device inference relates to low-latency real-time ML. WeepScope added back as 2nd project: 1D CNN+GRU on audio signals for cry classification maps directly to Nurix voice/audio ML stack; bullet reframed to emphasize audio classification rather than hardware. All work experience bullets retained as-is.

contacts.research_hook: Nurix featured Pushkar in their NEX video series on voice AI evaluation. He specifically argued that evaluating voice AI is fundamentally different from text LLMs -- latency, turn-taking, context retention, tone, recovery from failure, and behavior across multi-step workflows matter just as much as the final answer. He also reposted a paper on low-rank circuit conditioning for extracting and reusing neural network capabilities -- showing genuine interest in model internals and post-training. Background: Samsung Research, GSoC 2022, IIIT Vadodara. Working on inference engineering, kernels, agentic workflows, post-training at Nurix.
contacts.subject: voice AI eval beyond the final answer
contacts.content:
Hi Pushkar,

Caught your NEX video on voice AI evaluation a few weeks back. The argument you made -- that latency, turn-taking, and failure recovery are first-class evaluation signals, not just the final answer -- resonated. Most RAG evaluation I have seen in the wild still lives inside a text bubble.

I have been building production agentic RAG for the last two years: LangChain + LangGraph stateful orchestration handling permission checks, retrieval, tool calls, and answer generation in a single graph; 50K+ queries/month at 95% MRR after migrating off FAISS to Milvus; benchmarked 6 embedding models on live production data before settling on BGE-M3 with a reranker. The 'evaluate empirically on your actual distribution' instinct is the exact same loop I ran.

If your team is hiring on the ML side, I would be glad to talk. Does next Tuesday or Thursday work for 15 minutes?

Amit
amitgohel.dev | linkedin.com/in/amitgohel-ai
contacts.follow_up_1_subject: re: voice AI eval beyond the final answer
contacts.follow_up_1_content:
Hi Pushkar,

Following up in case this got buried. Shipped a short benchmark writeup on our 6-model embedding evaluation (BGE variants vs. Stella vs. SPLADE on production query data) -- happy to share if it is useful context. Either way, still interested in what your team is building on the inference and eval side at Nurix.

Amit
amitgohel.dev | linkedin.com/in/amitgohel-ai
contacts.follow_up_2_subject: re: voice AI eval beyond the final answer
contacts.follow_up_2_content:
Hi Pushkar,

Last note from me. If the timing is not right, no worries -- I would still appreciate being on your radar when it comes up.

Amit

contacts.research_hook: Building Voice AI at Nurix. Previously at Akamai (CDN edge infrastructure, sub-50ms global delivery) and Razorpay (high-throughput distributed payment systems). His infra background in low-latency delivery maps directly to the real-time constraints of voice AI. PES University, Bengaluru.
contacts.subject: Akamai edge instincts, voice AI latency
contacts.content:
Hi Amjad,

Your background caught my eye -- Akamai edge infrastructure to voice AI at Nurix is a specific path. The sub-50ms delivery instincts from CDN work are a different mental model than most ML engineers bring to latency problems in real-time voice systems.

I have been on the infra side of ML: cut RAG response time from 16s to 8s under 500+ concurrent users by migrating vector infra from FAISS to Milvus, then layering query preprocessing with intent identification on top. The production pressure is similar -- you have a hard real-time budget and cannot afford to debug latency only in the happy path.

If Nurix is hiring on the voice AI or inference side, I would like to hear more about how the stack is built. Open to a 15-minute call next week if you have a slot.

Amit
amitgohel.dev | linkedin.com/in/amitgohel-ai
contacts.follow_up_1_subject: re: Akamai edge instincts, voice AI latency
contacts.follow_up_1_content:
Hi Amjad,

Bumping this up. Since I sent the last email, I have been reading more about NuPlay's approach to interruption handling -- the gap between 'the model understood the interruption' and 'the system recovered cleanly at the infra level' is exactly the kind of problem your background seems built for. Still curious if there is a fit.

Amit
amitgohel.dev | linkedin.com/in/amitgohel-ai

contacts.follow_up_2_content:

Last note from me. If Nurix is not actively hiring right now, totally fine -- would still appreciate being on your radar for when it comes up.

Also reposts Nurix content on multi-agent orchestration and the full voice AI pipeline (ASR to NLU to process layer to NLG to TTS). CSE, BITS Pilani.
contacts.subject: multi-agent build beyond LLM-and-print
contacts.content:
Hi Tanmay,

Saw your post about the Nurix hackathon -- building a full multi-agent system with audio-video streaming coordination in 24 hours is a different bar than most hackathon outputs. The 'beyond call-the-LLM-and-print-response' framing is exactly right.

My production work is in the same space: LangGraph stateful graph handling permission checks, retrieval, tool calls, and answer generation in sequence; 50K+ queries/month at 95% MRR on a multi-tenant platform. The coordination layer is where the interesting problems live.

If there is an open ML or AI engineer role I should look at, I would appreciate a pointer. Happy to share more context on the work.

Amit

contacts.research_hook: Working on Nurix's sentinel layer for AI agent data privacy -- addressing how agent logs become PII black holes with SSNs, API secrets, and PHI cascading through chains. Nurix's approach uses NER plus regex techniques plus SHA-256 obfuscation. Security-conscious, privacy-first angle on production AI agents.
contacts.subject: agent log PII and the sentinel layer
contacts.content:
Hi Meghana,

Read about Nurix's sentinel layer for agent data privacy -- the framing of agent logs as PII black holes is accurate. SSNs and API secrets cascading through a multi-step chain before anyone notices is a real production problem, and the NER plus SHA-256 approach is the right direction.

I have been building production agentic pipelines with a similar awareness of what flows through the context: 250+ granular permissions per query enforced at retrieval time, with PostgreSQL as the ground truth for access control rather than trusting the LLM to self-filter. Milvus plus LangGraph handling the orchestration layer across 50K+ monthly queries.

If your team is hiring AI engineers, I'd be glad to talk. Open to a quick 15-minute call.

Amit
amitgohel.dev | linkedin.com/in/amitgohel-ai
contacts.follow_up_1_subject: re: agent log PII and the sentinel layer
contacts.follow_up_1_content:
Hi Meghana,

Quick follow-up. I have been thinking about the obfuscation tradeoff -- preserving enough context for debugging while still sanitizing is the hard part. Curious how your team handles that in practice. If there is an open role on your side, I would appreciate a pointer.

Amit
amitgohel.dev | linkedin.com/in/amitgohel-ai
contacts.follow_up_2_subject: re: agent log PII and the sentinel layer
contacts.follow_up_2_content:
Hi Meghana,

Last note from me. If the timing is not right, no worries -- happy to be on your radar whenever it comes up.

Amit

contacts.subject: Zigment to Nurix, same problem domain
contacts.content:
Hi Damodar,

The jump from Zigment to Nurix makes sense -- automated sales and customer engagement workflows are exactly what NuRep and NuPlay serve at the enterprise level. You moved into this problem from the product side before voice AI was the obvious next layer.

I have been building production AI for similar workflows: multi-model orchestration with GPT-4o, Qwen3-Coder, and Gemini for different subtask types; agentic RAG at 95% MRR on 50K+ monthly queries; AI-powered form pipelines processing 200+ PDFs in month one. Most of this is customer-facing automation in the same domain.

If there is an open AI engineer role at Nurix I should know about, I'd appreciate the pointer. Happy to share more.

Amit

contacts.subject: NEX ep 22 -- generic ASR and the custom stack
contacts.content:
Hi Rashi,

Saw you share the NEX episode on why generic ASR falls short for enterprises. The framing resonated -- the gap between a commodity ASR and a model actually trained on your domain, your speakers, your interruption patterns, and your latency budget is substantial.

I have been building production AI for two years: agentic RAG with LangChain and LangGraph (50K+ queries/month, 95% MRR), multi-model orchestration across GPT-4o, Qwen3-Coder, and Gemini for different subtask types, and low-latency inference engineering that cut response time from 16s to 8s under 500+ concurrent users. Earlier: IIT Delhi research on CNN-BiLSTM audio classification (1,000+ model configurations tested) and Samsung Solve for Tomorrow top 10 on on-device TinyML inference.

If your team is hiring on the ML or AI engineer side, I would like to hear more. Happy to talk for 15 minutes next week if you have a slot.

Amit
